en.text: . power: or, the power of the Holy Ghost coming upon you
. was: Gr. voice was made confounded: or, troubled in mind
when they had further threatened them
of the things that were sold
? to lie to: or, to deceive
: exceeding fair: or, fair to God
. miracles: Gr. signs and great miracles
. of this way: Gr. of the way
, as soon as I was sent for
rehearsed the matter from the beginning
when I had fastened mine eyes
. Easter: Gr. Passover quaternions: a file of four soldiers
. prayer: or, instant and earnest prayer was made
. to hearken: or, to ask who was there
that they should be put to death
country. was highly: or, bare an hostile mind, intending war the king's chamberlain: Gr. that was over the king's bedchamber
some to lead him by the hand
. suffered: or bore, or fed them as a nurse beareth, or feedeth, her child
of them which came up with
, in that he hath raised up
. mercies: Gr. holy, or just things: which word the Septuagint in many places, uses for that which is in the Hebrew, mercies
: after: or, after he had in his own age served the will of God
. the next: Gr. in the week between, or, in the sabbath between
unto the region that lieth round about
when they had preached the gospel
, had taught many: Gr. had made many disciples
. rose: or, rose up, said they, certain
, we came with a straight course
unto the things which were spoken
. whom: or, whom, said he, I preach
, is? Areopagus: or, Mars-hill: it was the highest court in Athens
. Mars' hill: or, court of the Areopagites
. devotions: or, gods that ye worship
men, in that he hath raised
. was Christ: or, is the Christ
, the chief ruler of the synagogue
, the chief ruler of the synagogue
. set at nought: or, brought into disrepute, or, contempt
of the image which fell down from Jupiter
? a worshipper: Gr. the temple keeper
. the law: or, the court days are kept
. abide me: or, wait for me
by the space of three years
when we had taken our leave
should have been pulled in pieces
. under a curse: or, with an oath of execration
. more: or, as some copies read, no more than eight or ten days
. I doubted: or, I was doubtful how to enquire hereof
. day and night: Gr. night and day
should it be thought a thing
of those things in the which
them, the fast: the fast was on the tenth day of the seventh month
. taken: or, cut the anchors, they left them in the sea
to the captain of the guard
